mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of rate-hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for mean return difference falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in > 50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which correlates with positive equity market momentum over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inflationary periods or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% while nonfarm payroll growth decelerates, monetary tightening outpaces labor market resilience, increasing recession probability and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in > 55% of triggered windows or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5% indicates severe demand contraction, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in > 65% of low-GDP quarters or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and force central bank tightening, systematically producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or 60-day returns are positive in > 58% of high-inflation observations across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payroll employment growth fuels household income expansion and consumer spending, directly supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained inflation episodes or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.3.
